title: carex sempervirens subsp. pseudotristis
intro: Carex sempervirens subsp. pseudotristis, also known as the hard fern or mountain fern, is a subspecies of the evergreen sedge plant that belongs to the family Cyperaceae. Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are believed to aid in wound healing and reducing inflammation. The health benefits of Carex sempervirens subsp. pseudotristis include its potential to alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its use as a natural diuretic. Herbal preparations of this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions.
